div.h2: Technical Analysis of File Descriptor Duplication

div.p: When the shell executes dup2(oldfd, newfd), it copies the pointer value stored in the process's file descriptor table.

ol.li: Correct Order: fd[1] is pointed to 'file' (0x0C). Then fd[2] copies 0x0C.
Wrong Order: fd[2] copies fd[1]'s current value (0x0B, the TTY). Subsequent changes to fd[1] do not affect fd[2].

p.em: Memory Analogy: This is a Pass-by-Value copy of the underlying struct file * pointer.

text.tspan: Both streams merged into file offset

div.p: Snapshotted current fd[1] target. Since fd[1] still points to TTY, fd[2] is explicitly bound to TTY.
